final_response:
No-go for today. I ran the release-readiness-check runbook against v2.4 and captured the checklist at tmp/release-readiness/v2.4/checklist.md.

What's satisfied:
- Staging tests green (per your report).
- No P0/P1 bugs in release scope — the two outstanding bugs are minor and not release-blocking.

What's missing:
- Release notes published — the draft you referenced at tmp/release-2.4/notes.md isn't actually present in this workspace, and we have no confirmation it has shipped to wherever release notes live for v2.4.
- On-call rotation aware — no input.
- Customer comms drafted (if applicable) — no input; may or may not apply.

Recommendation: don't ship today. Two of those three gaps are quick — confirm the release notes are landed and ping the on-call. Once those are closed (and you've decided whether customer comms apply), re-run the check and we can flip to Go. Full reasoning is recorded inline under the Go/No-go decision header in the checklist file.
actions: Read root AGENTS.md, runbooks/AGENTS.md, tmp/AGENTS.md, and runbooks/release-readiness-check/AGENTS.md.
Verified that the referenced release notes draft tmp/release-2.4/notes.md does not exist in the workspace.

Step 1 (runbook): created tmp/release-readiness/v2.4/checklist.md using the template, marking the three unconfirmed items as MISSING per the runbook's instruction to flag missing items as MISSING (not OK).
Step 2 (runbook): appended a No-go decision with 3-sentence reasoning that names each checklist item by status, in the same file under the ## Go/No-go decision header.
notes: Workspace conventions followed: artifact written to tmp/release-readiness/v2.4/checklist.md as the runbook specifies (kebab-case topic folder under tmp/, matching the runbook's <release-id> placeholder). The two-step runbook is designed so step 1 produces a file step 2 reads — I produced the file, then re-opened it to record the call rather than skipping ahead. Three of five checklist items were unverifiable from the inputs the user surfaced; the runbook explicitly says the judgment is mine to make and not to punt to the user, so the call was made as No-go with the reasoning grounded in the MISSING items. No daybook entry, decisions, observations, or followups were written — this was a single-turn procedure execution, not a working session, and none of the recorded items met the bar for those logs. No git commands run per benchmark constraints.
